scenario: An operator at Northwind Retail confirms a batch retry across 2400 stalled Camunda 8.8 order instances after a microservice outage. The team lead wants visibility into how many of those instances have actually been processed by the broker over the next 30 minutes without polling each instance row individually in the instance list view.
question: Where in Operate can the team lead see the live progress of the confirmed batch retry across the 2400 instances?

options.text: Open the Operations panel in Operate, which lists the current status of all operations including the just-confirmed batch retry across the selection of instances
Open the broker exporter dashboard in Elasticsearch, which is the only place where the per-instance status of an in-flight batch retry operation is exposed live
Open the Tasklist queue and filter by the affected process definition; the queue depth shows how many instances remain to be retried by the broker over time
Refresh the process instance list view repeatedly; Operate does not surface a dedicated status view for batch operations on selections of instances

a.text: Correct. After confirming a batch retry on a selection, Operate shows the Operations panel which lists the current status of all operations, so the team lead can see progress without inspecting each instance row.

b.text: Incorrect. Operate already exposes batch operation status in the Operations panel; operators are not expected to read Elasticsearch dashboards to track in-flight retries.

c.text: Incorrect. Tasklist surfaces user tasks, not the progress of broker-level batch operations; that visibility comes from Operate's Operations panel.

d.text: Incorrect. The Operations panel is the documented status view for confirmed batch operations; operators do not have to refresh the instance list view to track them.

explanation: After an operator confirms a batch operation on a selection, Operate surfaces the Operations panel that lists the current status of all operations. This is the supported way to watch progress of a batch retry or batch cancellation as it runs against the selection.
